# Solution

## (a) Implementation of `Canonicalize`

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width < PAD then PAD else x.width)
}
```

The function preserves the kind and value, and sets the width to the maximum of the current width and PAD (4).

## (b) Specification Lemma `Spec`

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var y := Canonicalize(x); 
          y.kind == x.kind &&
          y.value == x.value &&
          y.width >= PAD &&
          y.width >= NumDigits(y.value) &&
          (x.width >= PAD ==> y.width == x.width) &&
          Wellformed(y)
{ }
```

This captures precisely:
- **Kind and value preservation**: The kind and numeric value never change
- **Minimum width of 4**: The canonical width is at least PAD
- **Wellformedness**: The width remains sufficient to hold the numeric value
- **Idempotence**: If already at or above 4 digits, the width is unchanged
- **Output wellformedness**: The result is wellformed
